ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-07-2021 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Order Authorizing Guardian Ad Litem Timothy P. Breen To Execute And File Disclaimers On Behalf Of Minors Lucas Craig Barber And Eliza Jane Kathryne Barber
Off Calendar Feb-22-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Maureen McFadden appeared by zoom for Cheryl Rickards (Petitioner).
Christopher O'Connell appeared by zoom for Timothy Breen (Guardian Ad Litem for Eliza and Lucas).

The Court stated that Counsel made this more challenging because of the manner in which they chose to use the disclaimer, that it understands their position of wanting to honor the wishes of the will and that it needs to think about the significant advantage these funds could provide for the Minors. No one objects to the petition. The Court granted the Petition for Order Authorizing Guardian Ad Litem Timothy Breen to Execute and File Disclaimers on Behalf of Minors (order signed).

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Order Authorizing Guardian Ad Litem Timothy P. Breen To Execute And File Disclaimers On Behalf Of Minors Lucas Craig Barber And Eliza Jane Kathryne Barber
Off Calendar Jan-11-2021 Continued To Feb-22-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Maureen McFadden appeared by zoom for Cheryl Rickards (Petitioner/Administrator).
Christopher O'Connell appeared by zoom for Timothy Breen (Guardian Ad Litem for Eliza Barber and Lucas Barber (Minors)).

The Court stated that there is a procedural issue with the petition, that the declaration from the Guardian Ad Litem ("GAL") is from June, that the GAL was not appointed until August and that it is having trouble as to why it should exercise its discretion to take $140,000.00 away from a one year old and a six year old. The Court also stated that based on everything that is in this petition, it is not convinced by what has been presented. Ms. McFadden stated that although it is not a valid will, it was created prior to the birth of both of the Minors, that Lindsey, Tyler and Terry are the children of the Decedent's Brother, and that the Decedent did not provide for them in the will, but did provide for them by instruction to his Sister. Ms. McFadden also stated that even if they were born, the Decedent was very clear as to how he wanted his estate distributed and that the Mother of the Minors has no issue with it. The Court spoke of the significant affect it could have on the Minors and opportunities it could provide for them. Mr. O'Connell stated that he sees the Court's point, that the family sees that document as the Uncles' clear statement of his intent, that the family is wanting to honor the Decedent's intent and that the closest parallel is Probate Code section 15405 which allows a trust to be modified. The Court noted that disclaimers distinguish the interest of the person signing them, and then succession goes on. Ms. McFadden requested a continuance to provide further support of her petition. The Petition for Order Authorizing Guardian Ad Litem to Execute and File Disclaimers on Behalf of Minors is continued to February 22, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
